errorCodes.name: Control Board Fault
errorCodes.description: The main electronic control board (PCB) has failed. This board is the brain of your washing machine, controlling all cycles, water intake, and motor functions. When it fails, the washer may not start, display error codes, or behave erratically.

faqs.q: Can I repair the control board myself?
faqs.a: No. The control board requires professional repair or replacement. Attempting to repair printed circuit boards without proper equipment can cause further damage.

faqs.q: How much does a replacement control board cost?
faqs.a: Whirlpool main control boards typically cost $150-$300 for the part, plus $100-$200 labor if professionally installed.

faqs.q: What causes the control board to fail?
faqs.a: Power surges, water damage from leaks, age-related component failure, and electrical storms are common causes.

errorCodes.name: Drain Timeout
errorCodes.description: The drain pump did not remove water within the expected time (8 minutes). This typically indicates a clogged drain hose, faulty pump, or blocked filter preventing proper water evacuation.

faqs.a: If your washer is still under warranty, drain pump issues are typically covered. Check your warranty documentation.

faqs.q: Can I use the washer while this error displays?
faqs.a: No. Do not operate the washer with water remaining in the drum. This can cause mold and further damage.

errorCodes.name: Door Lock Fault
errorCodes.description: The door lock mechanism failed to secure the door after multiple attempts. This safety feature prevents the door from opening during operation.

errorCodes.name: Lock Error Motor
errorCodes.description: The motor has detected a locked rotor condition - the drum cannot rotate freely. This could be due to foreign objects (coins, buttons), motor failure, or drive system issues.

errorCodes.name: Overflow Error
errorCodes.description: Water level has exceeded the maximum safe limit. This serious condition could cause water overflow and floor damage if not addressed immediately.

errorCodes.name: Unbalance Error
errorCodes.description: The load is unbalanced during the spin cycle. The washer automatically reduces spin speed to prevent damage but cannot complete the cycle properly.

faqs.q: Why is my dryer taking too long?
faqs.a: Restricted airflow is the most common cause. Clean the lint trap, check the vent hose, and ensure the exterior vent flap opens properly.

faqs.q: Can I run dryer without vent?
faqs.a: No. Vented dryers require proper ventilation. Running without vent can cause fire hazard and damage the dryer.

faqs.q: Is the temperature sensor dangerous to replace?
faqs.a: Temperature sensors are generally safe to replace. Unplug the dryer and follow manufacturer instructions.
Age, moisture exposure, and electrical surges are common causes.

faqs.q: How do I test the heating element?
faqs.a: Use a multimeter to check for continuity. No continuity means the element is broken and needs replacement.

faqs.q: Water not filling - is it the inlet valve?
faqs.a: Most likely. Check water supply first, then test the inlet valve with a multimeter.
Common causes: clogged filter, kinked drain hose, faulty drain pump, or air gap blockage.
Yes. Water leaks can damage flooring and cause electrical hazards. Turn off power and water immediately.
The main board cannot communicate with the display board. Usually caused by loose wiring connections.
A noisy fan usually indicates failing motor. Replace soon to prevent further cooling issues.

faqs.q: Ice maker not making ice - common causes?
faqs.a: Check water supply, ice maker mold, and motor. Most common is frozen water line or stuck motor.
